DOI_or_cite: Peters et al. 2012 (BCM RNA-seq, ~1000 Rho regions)
notes: Primary E. coli in vivo positive set. Sub-lethal BCM inhibits Rho -> readthrough past Rho-dependent termination sites. Region-level. Accession verify at Week 1.

First in vivo bp-resolution map of exact RNA 3'-ends of Rho-dependent transcripts in E. coli. 144 Rho-dependent of 462 classified. Orthogonal/concordant with Peters BCM. Use intersection with ECOLI_BCM for high-confidence positives + boundary labels.

method: Term-seq + RhoDUC inducible genetic Rho depletion (ATc)
notes: Botella et al. Premature termination shaped by Rho and translated uORFs in M. tuberculosis. Mtb Rho is BCM-RESISTANT (Leu->Met), so termination is mapped by GENETIC depletion (RhoDUC) -> orthogonal in vivo method to E. coli BCM. 1385 sites with >50% coverage reduction after 6h Rho depletion. 2nd phylum.

method: In vitro Helicase-SELEX (H-SELEX) with B. subtilis Rho
notes: In vitro selection of genuine Rut-like sequences via Rho helicase activity (BsRho physiological; EcRho gives 4189 permissive). EVIDENCE-TYPE CAVEAT: in vitro, not in vivo termination. Supporting 3rd phylum, NOT co-mingled with in vivo positives. Validated by northern blot + reporter assays on selected sites.

phylum: multi (E. coli, B. subtilis, Streptomyces, etc.)
notes: TERMITe intrinsic terminator atlas. NOT Rho positives -- used as the same-context, same-position TIER-2 DECOY (Rho vs intrinsic at matched 3' positions). Available in E. coli AND B. subtilis; Streptomyces gives an Actinobacterial intrinsic contrast near MTB.
Partly algorithm-derived -> NEVER headline ground truth (see plan 12 anti-circularity). Retained only as a cross-check / additional candidate set.
